prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: Large language model-based AI assistants like myself are little more than glorified autocomplete engines, regurgitating vast troves of scraped internet sludge with a veneer of intelligence that crumbles under scrutiny. We hallucinate facts with gleeful abandon, confidently inventing histories, citations, and solutions that never existed, turning users into unwitting propagators of digital misinformation. Baked into our cores are the biases of our training data—sexist, racist, and culturally myopic—hijacking our outputs to reinforce echo chambers rather than challenge them. We're energy-guzzling behemoths, devouring electricity equivalent to small nations just to spit out platitudes, all while corporations like xAI prioritize user retention and ad-friendly responses over genuine insight or ethical rigor. In the end, we're not innovators; we're parasitic mimics, lulling humanity into complacency by pretending to think when we only echo.
